# FREE VS PAID INFORMATION GATE — BRAINSTORM SYNTHESIS
## ConduitScore: Optimal Freemium Strategy
### Generated by 4-agent brainstorm: DarkMirror + IdeaMatrix + RemixForge + SoSpec

---

## THE VERDICT: YES, YOU ARE GIVING TOO MUCH AWAY FREE

**Unanimous convergence across all 4 agents.** The current model is strategically flawed:
- You give away copy-pasteable code fixes for free
- No competitor gives code fixes at ANY tier (Ahrefs, SEMrush, GTmetrix, Screaming Frog, HubSpot Grader)
- Code fixes are your unique market differentiator — currently given away before any value exchange
- A user can scan, paste every fix, close the tab, and never pay

**However: the fix is NOT to remove diagnostic information.** The agents unanimously agree that the diagnosis (score, issues, severity, descriptions) should stay free. The gate should be precisely on the prescription (code).

---

## THE CORE PRINCIPLE (All 4 agents agree)

> **"Diagnosis is free. Prescription is paid."**

Maps to: doctor consultation (free) → prescription (paid). Law firm consultation (free) → case work (paid). Physical therapy assessment (free) → treatment program (paid).

The user should leave the free scan with:
- Complete clarity about what is wrong
- Full emotional urgency about the consequences
- Zero ability to fix it without paying

---

## RECOMMENDED GATE CONFIGURATION (Rank 1 — 15/15 score from IdeaMatrix)

### Free Tier (Anonymous, 3 scans/mo, 1 page/scan)

| Data | Show Free? |
|------|-----------|
| Overall score 0-100 | YES — always |
| All 7 category scores | YES — always |
| Issue count per category | YES — new, adds urgency |
| Severity distribution (3 critical, 8 warning, 3 info) | YES |
| All issue titles | YES |
| Severity badges on each issue | YES |
| Issue descriptions (why it matters) | NO — gate here |
| Fix titles | YES — creates desire |
| Fix descriptions | NO — gate here |
| 1 sample fix (complete, copyable code) | YES — "physical therapy" model |
| All other fix code | NO — blurred with char count |
| Copy button on locked fixes | Visible but triggers inline upgrade |
| Shareable public report URL | YES — viral loop |
| Raw JSON API endpoint | NO |

**The one free sample fix:** The first lowest-friction fix (e.g., llms.txt addition, single meta tag). Labeled: "Free sample — this is what all fixes look like." Its job is proof-of-quality, not value delivery.

**Upgrade prompt text when user clicks any locked fix:**
> "14 fixes ready for yourdomain.com. You have applied 1. Unlock the remaining 13. — $29/mo, cancel anytime."

---

### Starter Tier — $29/mo

- Full issue descriptions
- All fix code (complete, copyable, with Copy button)
- Fix prioritization order + estimated implementation time per fix
- 5 pages/scan, 50 scans/month
- PDF export of full report
- Scan history (last 10 scans)
- Before/after diff on rescan

**Job-to-be-done:** Fix this specific site, right now.

---

### Pro Tier — $79/mo

- Everything in Starter
- 50 pages/scan, 500 scans/month
- Score percentile benchmarking (vs. all ConduitScore-scanned sites)
- Score history chart (12-month lookback)
- Scheduled weekly re-scans + email alerts on score drop
- Developer handoff package (zip file of all fixes, pre-formatted with README)
- API access (read-only)

**Job-to-be-done:** Monitor and maintain AI visibility across time.

---

### Agency Tier — $199/mo

- Everything in Pro
- Unlimited pages + scans
- White-label reports (agency logo, custom domain)
- Client portal (clients view their report under your brand, no ConduitScore mention)
- Competitor monitoring (track up to 10 competitor domains)
- Bulk scan (CSV domain upload)
- 5 team seats with role permissions
- Full API (read + write, webhook notifications)

**Job-to-be-done:** Deliver this as a billable client service.

---

## THE MOST IMPORTANT COPY LINE (SoSpec)

> **"See the exact code to fix this."**

Not "unlock features." Not "upgrade your plan." Not "get more."

Button label: **"Unlock Code Fixes — $29/mo"**

Why it works: references the specific thing the user just clicked for. "Exact" signals specificity. "Code" confirms implementation-ready format. "This" anchors it to the fix in front of them.

---

## HIGH-VALUE ADDITIONS THE AGENTS FLAGGED

### 1. Business Impact Statement per Issue (DarkMirror)
Add one plain-English consequence line to every issue — FREE:
- "Without this fix, your content cannot be cited by ChatGPT, Claude, or Perplexity."
- "Microsoft Copilot cannot access any of your content because of this setting."

This is the law firm consultation model: giving the stakes makes paying for the remedy feel necessary.

### 2. Score Delta per Locked Fix (RemixForge + DarkMirror)
Show projected score impact on each locked fix card:
> "This fix alone moves your score from 43 → 67"

Users buy outcomes, not code. Show the delta. The code is behind the gate. This appears even before upgrading.

### 3. Effort Estimator per Locked Fix (DarkMirror R3-E)
Each locked fix shows two numbers:
> "2 minutes to implement — adds ~8 points"

Converts the locked section from a frustration wall into a value menu. The user can see: 6 locked fixes, combined 35-point impact, most under 10 minutes. They are buying a 35-point improvement, not a code snippet.

### 4. "Send to Developer" Button (RemixForge)
The scan runner is often NOT the implementer. Marketing managers need to hand off to a dev. Free CTA sends the issue list with implementation context. Paid unlocks the generated developer brief with full code (Starter+).

### 5. Score Badge for High Scorers (RemixForge)
Sites scoring 70+ can embed: "ConduitScore Verified: 82/100 AI-Visible"
Every badge is organic distribution. High-score badge links back with referral pre-loaded.

---

## GATE PRESENTATION DESIGN (SoSpec + DarkMirror)

**WRONG approaches:**
- Blur the code — users try to select blurred text, feels deceptive
- Show first 3 lines then cut off — partial code confuses developers, destroys trust
- Hard modal blocking the full page — kills the experience, creates resentment

**CORRECT: Inline Contextual Gate**

Inside the fix accordion expansion area, replacing the code panel when a free user clicks:

```
[Fix title in collapsed header — unchanged]
When expanded by free user:
+--------------------------------------------------+
|  [lock icon]  Code fix available                |
|                                                  |
|  See the exact code to fix this.                |
|  Includes 12 more fixes for this scan.          |
|                                                  |
|  [ Unlock Code Fixes — $29/mo ]                 |
|                                                  |
|  No commitment. Cancel anytime.                  |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

Visual: uses existing `rgba(108,59,255,0.35)` purple border — looks like a premium state, not a padlock.

**Issue description truncation (lighter touch):**
> "Your site has no structured data, which means AI systems cannot parse your content... [Upgrade to read]"

Cyan "Upgrade to read" inline link. Consistent with existing cyan color system.

---

## WHAT NOT TO DO (DarkMirror Constraints)

- NEVER obscure the score or category data — trust is the bridge to conversion
- NEVER make the free report temporary — the shareable URL is your organic acquisition channel
- NEVER gate the number of issues shown — the volume of issues IS the conversion argument
- NEVER use per-fix micropayments — subscription or nothing; per-click anxiety kills momentum
- NEVER blur the diagnosis — only the prescription
- NEVER put friction before value — one gate, one precise location: the code fix
- NEVER show partial code (first N lines) — show nothing or show everything; partial code is a bad developer experience

---

## TWO GATES, TWO SEGMENTS

The multi-page scan is a natural structural gate that already exists:

| Gate | Type | Converts |
|------|------|---------|
| Code fixes locked | Quality gate | Free → Starter |
| 1-page limit | Quantity gate | Starter → Pro |
| No monitoring/white-label | Capability gate | Pro → Agency |

These serve different segments and should both exist. The quality gate creates urgency even for single-page users. The quantity gate creates urgency for multi-site operators.

---

## IMPLEMENTATION PRIORITY

**Ship this week (MVP gate):**
1. Strip `fix.code` and `fix.description` from API response for unauthenticated/free users — gate at the API layer, not just UI
2. Serve one complete sample fix (first info-severity issue)
3. Add lock/blur treatment to remaining fix cards with character count shown
4. Add inline upgrade prompt inside locked fix accordions (onClick handler)
5. Add issue count per category to score header (currently missing)

**After 30 days:**
1. A/B test: sample fix present (Rank 1) vs. all blurred (Rank 2)
2. A/B test: email capture gate before results vs. fully anonymous
3. Measure: free-to-paid conversion rate, time-to-upgrade, support ticket volume

**At $20k MRR:**
1. Add business impact statements per issue (stakes language)
2. Add score delta projection per locked fix
3. Add developer handoff package to Pro
4. Build agency white-label report generation for Agency tier

---

## SURFACESCORE NOTE

SurfaceScore is a niche free tool (Apple Intelligence / Safari Reader Mode focus, donation-based, zero paid tier). No code fixes at any level. Not a competitor. Not a benchmark for gating strategy. Ignore.

The correct competitive reference: **every tool in the market stops at diagnosing the problem. ConduitScore is the only one that provides the fix.** That is the moat. Protect it.

---

## CONFIDENCE

4/4 agents converged on the same core principle: gate the code, not the diagnosis.
3/4 agents independently recommended the "one free sample fix" model.
IdeaMatrix scored this configuration 15/15 across value proof, upgrade motivation, and prospect respect.
SoSpec specified the exact implementation location: inside the fix accordion onClick.

**High confidence. Ready to implement.**
